title: This 22-year-old cancer survivor is running Boston for MGH
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Grace Taylor, 22, will run the Boston Marathon to honor Mass General Hospital's doctors and AYA program.
Taylor survived a rare, aggressive thyroid cancer diagnosed at 19, thanks to specialized treatment at MGH.
She joined a survivorship class at MGH, which provided support and connection during her cancer journey.

title: South Boston runner supports BMC social workers in Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Paige Beattie, 23, runs the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team BMC to support Boston Medical Center's safety-net mission.
She aims to raise $10,000 for BMC social workers who assist patients in crisis with housing and care coordination.
Beattie, from South Boston, drew inspiration from her mother and roommate, both involved in social work.

title: Patience remains an elusive virtue for Legacy FC attack
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC still seeks first NWSL win after a 1-0 loss to San Diego Wave at Gillette Stadium.
Coach Filipa Patão stresses patience amid team struggles, emphasizing calm and accuracy in attack phases.
Forward Amanda Gutierres, a former Brazilian star, yet to find the net in four appearances for Legacy FC.

title: Melrose man charged with stealing hundreds of thousands from clients, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Henry Rappa Jr., 57, was charged with embezzlement, larceny, and unauthorized law practice in Malden District Court.
Rappa allegedly stole $660,000 from a client, intending to safeguard it in a trust account during a legal dispute.
Despite being suspended, Rappa continued practicing law, handling a real estate closing and misappropriating $21,300.

title: Eugene Mirman posts message after fiery NH crash and dramatic rescue
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Eugene Mirman was rescued from a burning vehicle after crashing into the Bedford Toll Plaza in New Hampshire.
He was saved by a state trooper and two of Governor Ayotte’s security members; no charges have been filed.
Mirman thanked his rescuers and well-wishers on social media, following treatment for serious injuries at a local hospital.

title: NH trooper assaulted in unprovoked attack
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Jonathan G. Newcomb, 53, from Dover, NH, was arrested for assaulting a state trooper in an unprovoked attack.
The assault occurred in a parking lot off Dover Point Road near Route 16 while the trooper was in his parked cruiser.
Newcomb faces multiple charges, including felony criminal threatening and attempting to take a firearm from law enforcement.

title: This runner ‘barely finished’ the Boston Marathon last year. Now he’s back to run again.
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Bryan Mallett, 24, from Lynnfield, runs the Boston Marathon to support Mass General after losing his second mother to cancer.
Mallett's second mother, Sharla Sieve, passed from metastatic colorectal cancer; a fundraiser in her honor raised over $123,000.
Mallett, a videographer for the Boston Bruins, aims to complete all major marathons, inspired by Sieve's active lifestyle.

title: NFL executives reportedly ‘expect’ Patriots to acquire A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: NFL executives expect the Patriots to acquire Eagles wide receiver A.J. Brown after June 1 for salary-cap reasons.
The potential trade hinges on Philadelphia's cap savings, reducing Brown's dead cap hit from $43 million to $16.4 million post-June 1.
Eagles reportedly seek a first-round draft pick and possibly more in exchange for A.J. Brown, with no deal finalized yet.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Amanda Seyfried stars in 'The Testament of Ann Lee,' streaming on Hulu.
'28 Years Later: The Bone Temple,' a sequel directed by Nia DaCosta, is streaming on Netflix.
Ken Burns' documentary 'Henry David Thoreau,' featuring Don Henley, streams on PBS.

title: Caleb Durbin’s redemption, Willson Contreras’s swagger & other takeaways from Red Sox’ home-opening win
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox defeat Padres 5-2 in home opener, snapping a five-game losing streak at Fenway Park.
Caleb Durbin breaks an 0-for-34 slump with an RBI single, earning cheers from Fenway fans.
Johan Oviedo placed on 15-day injured list due to a pitching elbow strain, awaiting further evaluation.
